title: Skill: AI-agent-friendly corvee installer
description: Author a Claude Code skill (skills/corvee-install/SKILL.md) that walks an AI agent through installing corvee from scratch. Companion to the existing skills/corvee/SKILL.md (which documents the agent claim/heartbeat/done loop and assumes the binary is already installed). Trigger phrases include 'install corvee', 'setup corvee', 'add corvee to this project'. The skill should: (1) detect the host OS and architecture; (2) prefer 'go install github.com/.../cmd/corvee@latest' when Go ≥1.26 is available; (3) fall back to downloading the matching pre-built release binary into /usr/local/bin (or ~/.local/bin if non-root); (4) verify 'corvee version' resolves on PATH; (5) ask the agent for a project code and run 'corvee init'; (6) print the agent loop summary so the LLM knows what to do next. Ship as part of the skills tarball that 'make skill-tarball' produces today.
acceptance_criteria: skills/corvee-install/SKILL.md exists with trigger metadata and step-by-step prose
falls back to release binary download
Skill verifies corvee version after install and aborts cleanly on failure
Skill bootstraps .tasks/ via corvee init using a user-supplied project code
End-to-end smoke test: fresh shell + clean repo + agent triggers install skill → corvee version exits 0 and .tasks/ exists

journal.note: Skill scaffold: skills/corvee-install/{SKILL.md,README.md} bundled in skill-tarball; install/verify/init steps with OS-arch detection, go install preferred, release binary fallback, source build last resort.
